alzio.detect 0.0.0.9000
  • Reference
  • Articles
    • Introduction to alzio.detect

    Classify cognitive staging

    classify_cognitive_stage.Rd

    Assigns a cognitive stage based on clinical and biomarker data using the NIA-AA research framework (Jack et al., 2018) and the preclinical AD staging scheme (Sperling et al., 2011).

    classify_cognitive_stage(mmse, cdr_global, abeta_status, tau_status)

    Arguments

    mmse

    MMSE total score (0–30). Used for consistency checks; CDR takes precedence for staging.

    cdr_global

    Global CDR score (0–3).

    abeta_status

    Amyloid status: "positive" or "negative".

    tau_status

    Tau status: "positive" or "negative" (used for preclinical AD sub-staging in future versions).

    Value

    A factor with levels "cognitively_normal", "preclinical_ad", "mci", "dementia".

    Details

    Staging logic:

    • Cognitively normal: CDR = 0, amyloid negative.

    • Preclinical AD: CDR = 0, amyloid positive (Sperling et al., 2011 Stages 1–3).

    • MCI: CDR = 0.5, amyloid positive.

    • Dementia: CDR \(\ge\) 1, amyloid positive.

    If biomarkers are "negative", the MCI and dementia stages are classified as non-AD cognitive impairment (returned as "cognitively_normal" for CDR = 0, or the clinical stage with a warning).

    References

    Jack CR Jr, Bennett DA, Blennow K, et al. (2018) NIA-AA Research Framework: Toward a biological definition of Alzheimer's disease. Alzheimer's & Dementia 14(4):535–562. doi:10.1016/j.jalz.2018.02.018

    Sperling RA, Aisen PS, Beckett LA, et al. (2011) Toward defining the preclinical stages of Alzheimer's disease: recommendations from the National Institute on Aging-Alzheimer's Association workgroups on diagnostic guidelines for Alzheimer's disease. Alzheimer's & Dementia 7(3):280–292. doi:10.1016/j.jalz.2011.03.003

    Perneczky R, Wagenpfeil S, Komossa K, et al. (2006) Mapping scores onto stages: Mini-Mental State Examination and Clinical Dementia Rating. American Journal of Geriatric Psychiatry 14(2):139–144. doi:10.1097/01.JGP.0000192478.82189.a8
